claim: Agentic workflows outperform zero-shot prompting significantly: GPT-3.5 with agentic reasoning achieves ~95% on HumanEval coding benchmark vs. 48% with zero-shot
actionable: Use agentic workflows (reflection, planning, tool-use) for complex tasks; expect 2-3x improvement over single-turn prompting, especially for coding and analysis.
source_title: Andrew Ng Explores The Rise Of AI Agents And Agentic Reasoning

claim: Reflection pattern: prompt LLM to critique its own output, feed criticism back as feedback, iterate to improve code/output quality
actionable: Implement reflection loops: have LLM generate code, critique it, then revise; add unit test feedback to further improve; often yields measurable quality gains without model upgrades.
source_title: Andrew Ng Explores The Rise Of AI Agents And Agentic Reasoning

claim: Tool-use design pattern: LLM decides when to call functions, search web, execute code, issue refunds, send emails, or access calendars
actionable: Design tools with clear interfaces; enable LLM to decide which tool to call and when; common tools: web search, code execution, API calls, calendar access, email triggers.
source_title: Andrew Ng Explores The Rise Of AI Agents And Agentic Reasoning

claim: Planning/reasoning pattern: LLM breaks complex requests into sequence of actions (detect pose, generate image, describe, convert to audio) and executes in order
actionable: For multi-step tasks (video generation, image analysis, content creation), instruct LLM to plan steps first, then execute; use intermediate outputs as context for next step.
source_title: Andrew Ng Explores The Rise Of AI Agents And Agentic Reasoning

claim: Multi-agent collaboration: prompt single LLM to play different roles (coder, critic, reviewer) in sequence; LLMs show better results with this decomposition
actionable: For complex tasks, have LLM alternate roles: generate code, critique as reviewer, revise; similar to parallel processing extraction for developers—breaks work into subtasks.
source_title: Andrew Ng Explores The Rise Of AI Agents And Agentic Reasoning

claim: Vision agents enable agentic workflows on images and video: detect objects, analyze frames, plan steps, generate code to extract metadata
actionable: Use vision agents to process video data: detect players in sports footage, identify scene boundaries, extract frame metadata, generate searchable video indexes.
source_title: Andrew Ng Explores The Rise Of AI Agents And Agentic Reasoning

claim: Fast ML development via generative AI: sentiment classification can go from 6-12 months (supervised learning) to ~10 days with prompt-based approach
actionable: For classification/analysis tasks, start with prompt-based generative approach, not supervised learning pipelines; validate on small sets, iterate quickly.
source_title: Andrew Ng Explores The Rise Of AI Agents And Agentic Reasoning

claim: LLM-based applications need more robust evals than supervised learning; test collection should happen in parallel with prototyping, not sequentially
actionable: Shift evaluation strategy: collect test data in parallel with building prototypes; don't wait for full build before testing—bottleneck is evals, not models.
source_title: Andrew Ng Explores The Rise Of AI Agents And Agentic Reasoning

claim: Agentic orchestration layer (e.g., LangChain, LandingAI Vision Agent) makes building rich agents easier; abstracts complexity of tool-calling and planning
actionable: Use agentic orchestration frameworks instead of building agents from scratch; these frameworks handle reflection loops, tool-calling, planning, and error recovery.
source_title: Andrew Ng Explores The Rise Of AI Agents And Agentic Reasoning

claim: Move fast and be responsible: teams can prototype quickly while evaluating robustly—don't ship untested; maintain guardrails and responsible deployment
actionable: Adopt 'move fast and be responsible' mindset: build rapidly, test thoroughly before shipping; implement guardrails, safety checks, and responsible deployment practices.
source_title: Andrew Ng Explores The Rise Of AI Agents And Agentic Reasoning
